items.claim: Dustin Jacoby has surprising power despite not looking like a traditional power puncher.
items.quote: People always forget that Dustin Jacoby's powerful because he doesn't look like a power puncher, but the dude has a ton of knockouts.

items.claim: Dustin Jacoby effectively transitions from stuffing takedowns immediately into striking combinations.
items.quote: as soon as he stuffs a takedown, he goes right into striking which makes it really hard for people who want to continually shoot takedowns.

items.claim: Sam Patterson has a defensive flaw where he keeps his chin exposed while striking.
items.quote: he does just kind of have his chin up there to be hit.

items.claim: Sam Patterson has a significant grappling advantage over Santiago Ponzinibbio.
items.quote: if he gets this fight to the mat, I I he has a big big time advantage over a guy like Ponzinibbio.

items.claim: Santiago Ponzinibbio has not been the same fighter since suffering a major injury several years ago.
items.quote: ever since Ponzi had that big injury like few years ago, more than a few years ago, quite a while ago, he never quite came back the same.

items.claim: Mike Davis is the superior striker in his matchup against Nurullo Aliev.
items.quote: He's definitely the better striker in this match up.

items.claim: Mike Davis has a tendency to throw high-impact kicks as opponents attempt to exit the pocket.
items.quote: as his opponent tries to exit the pocket, that's when he throws a big kick

items.claim: Nurullo Aliev's striking is primarily effective because his grappling threat forces opponents to respect his offense.
items.quote: It's basically the grappling threat sets it up and it sets up his flurries.

items.claim: Magomed Tuqayev leaves himself vulnerable by blitzing with his hands down and leading with his head.
items.quote: sometimes he does these blitzes where his hands are down here and he'll lead with the head and start throwing.

items.claim: Brenton Hibero is overly willing to accept bottom position while searching for low-percentage submissions.
items.quote: he's willing to accept the bottom for too long. It's frustrating cuz I know the guy has sweeps.

items.claim: Axel Solla uses leg attacks primarily to create space to return to his feet rather than to secure submissions.
items.quote: he often uses leg attacks to create space. Now, he's not getting these leg attacks, but he's threatening them enough that people have to at least acknowledge them

items.claim: Ismael Bonfim has a tendency to mentally break during fights.
items.quote: he's technically the better, more skilled fighter here. But he breaks.
